candidates.reasoning: The vast, semi-arid high plains landscape with dry yellow grasses and rolling hills is highly characteristic of the American West (e.g., Wyoming, Montana, or Colorado). The road infrastructure, specifically the yellow center line and white shoulder lines, is a standard North American pattern.
The prairie landscape of Alberta or Saskatchewan shares nearly identical terrain, soil color, and vegetation with the US Great Plains. The road markings are also consistent with Canadian standards.

The wide-open steppe and arid environment are geographically similar, though the high quality of the paved road and specific line markings are less common in remote Mongolian regions compared to North America.
The Central Asian steppe offers similar flat, dry grassland and rolling hills, but the specific road marking style is more indicative of North American standards.

evidence: Yellow center line and white edge lines on asphalt
Rolling hills with sparse tree cover
Northern hemisphere sun angle based on shadow projection

candidates.reasoning: The landscape consists of semi-arid shortgrass prairie typical of the Great Plains or Intermountain West. However, this vegetation is cosmopolitan for temperate grasslands and not diagnostic enough to distinguish from Canada or Mongolia.
The dry, golden grassland is consistent with the Canadian Prairies (Alberta/Saskatchewan). Like the USA, the lack of specific endemic species makes this a low-confidence botanical identification.
The vast, treeless steppe with dormant yellow grasses is characteristic of the Central Asian steppe. There are no specific shrubs or flora visible to differentiate this from North American prairies.

The combination of right-hand traffic and a solid yellow center line is a primary characteristic of US road infrastructure. The white fog lines on the edges are also standard for American rural highways.

Canada shares nearly identical road marking standards with the USA, including right-hand traffic and yellow center lines. It is indistinguishable from the USA based solely on these infrastructure features.

Mexico drives on the right and uses yellow center lines, though the specific quality and style of the road shoulder and markings seen here are more typical of North American (US/Canada) rural standards.

The thin, metal delineator posts with small white reflectors on the left side of the road are very common in rural US states. The style of the wooden post-and-rail fencing on the left is also characteristic of North American ranching areas.
The delineator posts and rural fencing styles are nearly identical to those found in the USA, making it a strong candidate for the prairie provinces.

evidence: thin metal delineator posts with white reflectors

moderator_decisions.reasoning: All agents with medium or higher confidence agree on the United States. The only agent with a different confidence level (botanics) still agrees on the country.
moderator_decisions.termination_reason: All agents with confidence >= 'medium' agree on the same top country (consensus).
